text: U.S. container imports fall in October amid tariff-driven caution. Why are clinical trials still dominated by men? Gender gap raises concern. A line of people rolled their suitcases out of a clinical trial center in Seoul’s Gangseo District on Oct. 18. Leading the group was a young man wearing a black cap pulled low. Several men in hoodies and athletic wear followed. By the time the last participant walked out, 50 people had exited the building. All of them were men. According to the Ministry of Food and Drug Safety, clinical trial participants last year included about 92,600 men and about 68,600 women, showing a clear gap of more than 30 percent. The pattern was the same in previous years. In 2022, about 90,000 men participated compared with about 65,500 women. In 2023, about 89,500 men participated compared with about 67,100 women. An investigation by the Hankook Ilbo found similar results. Reporters visited about 20 hospitals conducting clinical trials for healthy volunteers more than 20 times in August and observed that most centers were filled with male participants. Marathon runner left brain-dead after being hit by truck during race. A runner in his twenties was struck by a truck during a marathon in North Chungcheong Province, leaving him brain-dead. According to police and fire authorities, the crash occurred at about 10:08 a.m., Monday, in Okcheon County during a marathon event. A 1-ton Porter truck hit the runner, a 25-year-old athlete from the Cheongju City Hall team. The accident happened when the truck, traveling in the first lane, suddenly changed into the second lane. The race course had two lanes: one remained open to regular traffic and the other was reserved for marathon runners. A was running at the front of the pack, with a police vehicle escorting runners about 20 to 30 meters ahead. The North Chungcheong Sports Council said the runner suffered severe head injuries and was taken to a general hospital in Daejeon, where he was later declared brain-dead and placed on life support. He joined the Cheongju City Hall team in August and had recently recorded strong results in multiple races.
